ipc_sections.title: Committing Depredation on Territories of Power at Peace with Government of India
ipc_sections.description: Whoever commits depredation, or makes preparations to commit depredation, on the territories of any Power in alliance or at peace with the Government of India.

ipc_sections.title: Threat of Injury to Public Servant
ipc_sections.description: Whoever holds out any threat of injury to any public servant, or to any person in whom that public servant is interested, for the purpose of inducing that public servant to do any act, or to forbear or delay to do any act, connected with the exercise of the public functions of such public servant.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both

sub_sections.description: Whoever holds out any threat of injury to any public servant or to any person in whom such public servant is interested, with intent to induce that public servant to do any act connected with the exercise of his public functions and such act is of a criminal nature, shall be punished with imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both.

ipc_sections.title: Giving False Evidence
ipc_sections.description: Whoever, being legally bound by an oath or by an express provision of law to state the truth, or being bound by law to make a declaration upon any subject, makes any statement which is false, and which he either knows or believes to be false or does not believe to be true, is said to give false evidence.

sub_sections.description: A statement is within the meaning of this section whether it is made verbally or otherwise, whether it is made in a judicial proceeding or in any other proceeding before any authority lawfully empowered to receive evidence or before any other person required or authorised by law to take such declaration.

ipc_sections.title: Committing Depredation on Territories of Power at Peace with Government of India
ipc_sections.description: Whoever commits depredation, or makes preparations to commit depredation, on the territories of any Power in alliance or at peace with the Government of India.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years and fine and forfeiture of property

sub_sections.description: Whoever makes preparation to commit depredation on territories of a Power at peace with the Government of India shall be punished with imprisonment up to 7 years and fine and forfeiture of property used or intended to be used.

ipc_sections.title: Threat of Injury to Public Servant
ipc_sections.description: Whoever holds out any threat of injury to any public servant, or to any person in whom that public servant is interested, for the purpose of inducing that public servant to do any act, or to forbear or delay to do any act, connected with the exercise of the public functions of such public servant.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both

sub_sections.description: Whoever holds out any threat of injury to any public servant or to any person in whom such public servant is interested, with intent to induce that public servant to do any act connected with the exercise of his public functions and such act is of a criminal nature, shall be punished with imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both.

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for Wrongful Restraint
ipc_sections.description: Whoever wrongfully restrains any person shall be punished with simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to one month, or with fine which may extend to five hundred rupees, or with both.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Simple imprisonment up to 1 month or fine up to Rs. 500 or both

ipc_sections.title: Various Offenses / Other Clauses
ipc_sections.description: Details not explicitly found in standard directory.

ipc_sections.title: Assault or Criminal Force with Intent to Dishonour Person
ipc_sections.description: Whoever assaults or uses criminal force to any person, intending thereby to dishonour that person, otherwise than on grave and sudden provocation given by that person.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both

ipc_sections.title: Voluntarily Causing Hurt by Dangerous Weapons or Means
ipc_sections.description: Whoever, except in the case provided for by section 334, voluntarily causes hurt by means of any instrument for shooting, stabbing or cutting, or any instrument which, used as a weapon of offence, is likely to cause death, or by means of fire or any heated substance, or by means of any poison or any corrosive substance, or by means of any explosive substance.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years or fine or both

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for Criminal Intimidation
ipc_sections.description: Whoever commits the offence of criminal intimidation shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both; and if the threat be to cause death or grievous hurt, or to cause the destruction of any property by fire, or to cause an offence punishable with death or imprisonment for life, or with imprisonment for a term which may extend to seven years, or to impute unchastity to a woman, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to seven years, or with fine, or with both.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both; 7 years or fine or both if threat is of death or grievous hurt etc.

ipc_sections.title: Various Offenses / Other Clauses
ipc_sections.description: Details not explicitly found in standard directory.

ipc_sections.title: Committing Depredation on Territories of Power at Peace with Government of India
ipc_sections.description: Whoever commits depredation, or makes preparations to commit depredation, on the territories of any Power in alliance or at peace with the Government of India.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years and fine and forfeiture of property

sub_sections.description: Whoever makes preparation to commit depredation on territories of a Power at peace with the Government of India shall be punished with imprisonment up to 7 years and fine and forfeiture of property used or intended to be used.

ipc_sections.title: Abetment of Offence Punishable with Death or Imprisonment for Life — If Offence Not Committed
ipc_sections.description: Whoever abets the commission of an offence punishable with death or imprisonment for life, shall, if that offence be not committed in consequence of the abetment, and no express provision is made for the punishment of such abetment, be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to seven years, and shall also be liable to fine.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years and fine; 14 years and fine if hurt caused

sub_sections.description: If any act for which the abettor is liable in consequence of the abetment, and which causes hurt to any person, is done, the abettor shall be liable to imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to fourteen years, and shall also be liable to fine.

ipc_sections.title: Assault
ipc_sections.description: Whoever makes any gesture, or any preparation intending or knowing it to be likely that such gesture or preparation will cause any person present to apprehend that he who makes that gesture or preparation is about to use criminal force to that person, is said to commit an assault.

ipc_sections.description: In executing a sentence of solitary confinement, such confinement shall in no case exceed fourteen days at a time, with intervals between the periods of solitary confinement of not less than such periods.

ipc_sections.description: Whoever, having been convicted of an offence punishable under Chapter XII or XVII with three years' imprisonment or upwards, is afterwards guilty of any offence punishable under either of those Chapters with imprisonment, shall be subject to enhanced punishment.

ipc_sections.description: Nothing is an offence which is done by any person who is justified by law, or who by reason of a mistake of fact in good faith believes himself to be justified by law.

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for Criminal Breach of Trust
ipc_sections.description: Whoever commits criminal breach of trust shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three years, or with fine, or with both.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years or fine or both

ipc_sections.title: Husband or Relative of Husband of a Woman Subjecting Her to Cruelty
ipc_sections.description: Whoever, being the husband or the relative of the husband of a woman, subjects such woman to cruelty. 'Cruelty' includes wilful conduct likely to drive the woman to commit suicide or cause grave injury; or harassment with a view to coercing her to meet any unlawful demand for dowry.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years and fine

ipc_sections.title: Various Offenses / Other Clauses
ipc_sections.description: Details not explicitly found in standard directory.

ipc_sections.title: Threat of Injury to Public Servant
ipc_sections.description: Whoever holds out any threat of injury to any public servant, or to any person in whom that public servant is interested, for the purpose of inducing that public servant to do any act, or to forbear or delay to do any act, connected with the exercise of the public functions of such public servant.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both

sub_sections.description: Whoever holds out any threat of injury to any public servant or to any person in whom such public servant is interested, with intent to induce that public servant to do any act connected with the exercise of his public functions and such act is of a criminal nature, shall be punished with imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both.

ipc_sections.title: Assault or Criminal Force to Deter Public Servant from Discharge of His Duty
ipc_sections.description: Whoever assaults or uses criminal force to any person being a public servant in the execution of his duty, or with intent to prevent or deter that person from discharging his duty.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for Wrongful Confinement
ipc_sections.description: Whoever wrongfully confines any person shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to one year, or with fine which may extend to one thousand rupees, or with both.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 1 year or fine up to Rs. 1000 or both

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for Criminal Intimidation
ipc_sections.description: Whoever commits the offence of criminal intimidation shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both; and if the threat be to cause death or grievous hurt, or to cause the destruction of any property by fire, or to cause an offence punishable with death or imprisonment for life, or with imprisonment for a term which may extend to seven years, or to impute unchastity to a woman, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to seven years, or with fine, or with both.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both; 7 years or fine or both if threat is of death or grievous hurt etc.

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for Being Member of Unlawful Assembly
ipc_sections.description: Whoever is a member of an unlawful assembly shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to six months, or with fine, or with both.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: 6 months imprisonment or fine or both

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for Public Nuisance in Cases Not Otherwise Provided For
ipc_sections.description: Whoever commits a public nuisance in any case not otherwise punishable by this Code.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Fine up to Rs. 200

ipc_sections.description: Whoever, knowing that, by an order promulgated by a public servant lawfully empowered to promulgate such order, he is directed to abstain from a certain act or to take certain order with certain property in his possession or under his management, disobeys such direction.

ipc_sections.title: Committing Depredation on Territories of Power at Peace with Government of India
ipc_sections.description: Whoever commits depredation, or makes preparations to commit depredation, on the territories of any Power in alliance or at peace with the Government of India.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years and fine and forfeiture of property

sub_sections.description: Whoever makes preparation to commit depredation on territories of a Power at peace with the Government of India shall be punished with imprisonment up to 7 years and fine and forfeiture of property used or intended to be used.

ipc_sections.title: Threat of Injury to Public Servant
ipc_sections.description: Whoever holds out any threat of injury to any public servant, or to any person in whom that public servant is interested, for the purpose of inducing that public servant to do any act, or to forbear or delay to do any act, connected with the exercise of the public functions of such public servant.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both

sub_sections.description: Whoever holds out any threat of injury to any public servant or to any person in whom such public servant is interested, with intent to induce that public servant to do any act connected with the exercise of his public functions and such act is of a criminal nature, shall be punished with imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both.

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for Rioting
ipc_sections.description: Whoever is guilty of rioting shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: 2 years imprisonment or fine or both

ipc_sections.title: Rioting Armed with Deadly Weapon
ipc_sections.description: Whoever is guilty of rioting, being armed with a deadly weapon or with anything likely to cause death.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: 3 years imprisonment or fine or both

ipc_sections.title: Punishment of Abetment if Act Abetted Is Committed in Consequence and Where No Provision Is Made
ipc_sections.description: Whoever abets any offence shall, if the act abetted is committed in consequence of the abetment, and no express provision is made for the punishment of such abetment, be punished with the punishment provided for the offence.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Same as for the offence abetted

ipc_sections.title: Obscene Acts and Songs
ipc_sections.description: Whoever, to the annoyance of others, does any obscene act in any public place, or sings, recites or utters any obscene song, ballad or words, in or near any public place.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 months or fine or both

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for Voluntarily Causing Hurt
ipc_sections.description: Whoever, except in the case provided for by section 334, voluntarily causes hurt, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to one year, or with fine which may extend to one thousand rupees, or with both.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 1 year or fine up to Rs. 1000 or both

ipc_sections.title: Voluntarily Causing Hurt by Dangerous Weapons or Means
ipc_sections.description: Whoever, except in the case provided for by section 334, voluntarily causes hurt by means of any instrument for shooting, stabbing or cutting, or any instrument which, used as a weapon of offence, is likely to cause death, or by means of fire or any heated substance, or by means of any poison or any corrosive substance, or by means of any explosive substance.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years or fine or both

ipc_sections.title: Attempt to Murder
ipc_sections.description: Whoever does any act with such intention or knowledge, and under such circumstances that, if he by that act caused death, he would be guilty of murder, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to ten years, and shall also be liable to fine.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 10 years and fine; imprisonment for life if hurt is caused

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for Criminal Intimidation
ipc_sections.description: Whoever commits the offence of criminal intimidation shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both; and if the threat be to cause death or grievous hurt, or to cause the destruction of any property by fire, or to cause an offence punishable with death or imprisonment for life, or with imprisonment for a term which may extend to seven years, or to impute unchastity to a woman, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to seven years, or with fine, or with both.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both; 7 years or fine or both if threat is of death or grievous hurt etc.

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for Rioting
ipc_sections.description: Whoever is guilty of rioting shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: 2 years imprisonment or fine or both

ipc_sections.title: Obscene Acts and Songs
ipc_sections.description: Whoever, to the annoyance of others, does any obscene act in any public place, or sings, recites or utters any obscene song, ballad or words, in or near any public place.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 months or fine or both

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for Voluntarily Causing Hurt
ipc_sections.description: Whoever, except in the case provided for by section 334, voluntarily causes hurt, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to one year, or with fine which may extend to one thousand rupees, or with both.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 1 year or fine up to Rs. 1000 or both

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for Wrongful Restraint

ipc_sections.title: Giving False Evidence
ipc_sections.description: Whoever, being legally bound by an oath or by an express provision of law to state the truth, or being bound by law to make a declaration upon any subject, makes any statement which is false, and which he either knows or believes to be false or does not believe to be true, is said to give false evidence.

sub_sections.description: A statement is within the meaning of this section whether it is made verbally or otherwise, whether it is made in a judicial proceeding or in any other proceeding before any authority lawfully empowered to receive evidence or before any other person required or authorised by law to take such declaration.

ipc_sections.title: Various Offenses / Other Clauses
ipc_sections.description: Details not explicitly found in standard directory.

ipc_sections.title: Abetment of Offence Punishable with Death or Imprisonment for Life — If Offence Not Committed
ipc_sections.description: Whoever abets the commission of an offence punishable with death or imprisonment for life, shall, if that offence be not committed in consequence of the abetment, and no express provision is made for the punishment of such abetment, be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to seven years, and shall also be liable to fine.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years and fine; 14 years and fine if hurt caused

sub_sections.description: If any act for which the abettor is liable in consequence of the abetment, and which causes hurt to any person, is done, the abettor shall be liable to imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to fourteen years, and shall also be liable to fine.

ipc_sections.title: Assault
ipc_sections.description: Whoever makes any gesture, or any preparation intending or knowing it to be likely that such gesture or preparation will cause any person present to apprehend that he who makes that gesture or preparation is about to use criminal force to that person, is said to commit an assault.

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for Murder by Life-Convict
ipc_sections.description: Whoever, being under sentence of imprisonment for life, commits murder, shall be punished with death.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Death

ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 6 months or fine up to Rs. 1000 or both

ipc_sections.title: Using Evidence Known to Be False
ipc_sections.description: Whoever corruptly uses or attempts to use as true or genuine evidence any evidence which he knows to be false or fabricated.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Same as for giving false evidence (IPC 193)

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for Assault or Criminal Force Otherwise Than on Grave Provocation
ipc_sections.description: Whoever assaults or uses criminal force to any person otherwise than on grave and sudden provocation given by that person.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 months or fine up to Rs. 500 or both

ipc_sections.title: Assault or Criminal Force to Deter Public Servant from Discharge of His Duty
ipc_sections.description: Whoever assaults or uses criminal force to any person being a public servant in the execution of his duty, or with intent to prevent or deter that person from discharging his duty.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both

ipc_sections.title: Assault or Criminal Force to Deter Public Servant from Discharge of His Duty
ipc_sections.description: Whoever assaults or uses criminal force to any person being a public servant in the execution of his duty, or with intent to prevent or deter that person from discharging his duty.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both

ipc_sections.title: Various Offenses / Other Clauses
ipc_sections.description: Details not explicitly found in standard directory.

ipc_sections.title: Committing Depredation on Territories of Power at Peace with Government of India
ipc_sections.description: Whoever commits depredation, or makes preparations to commit depredation, on the territories of any Power in alliance or at peace with the Government of India.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years and fine and forfeiture of property

sub_sections.description: Whoever makes preparation to commit depredation on territories of a Power at peace with the Government of India shall be punished with imprisonment up to 7 years and fine and forfeiture of property used or intended to be used.

ipc_sections.title: Threat of Injury to Public Servant
ipc_sections.description: Whoever holds out any threat of injury to any public servant, or to any person in whom that public servant is interested, for the purpose of inducing that public servant to do any act, or to forbear or delay to do any act, connected with the exercise of the public functions of such public servant.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both

sub_sections.description: Whoever holds out any threat of injury to any public servant or to any person in whom such public servant is interested, with intent to induce that public servant to do any act connected with the exercise of his public functions and such act is of a criminal nature, shall be punished with imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both.

ipc_sections.title: Sale of Obscene Objects to Young Person
ipc_sections.description: Whoever sells, lets to hire, distributes, exhibits or circulates to any person under the age of twenty years any such obscene object as is referred to in the last preceding section.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years and fine up to Rs. 2000; 7 years and Rs. 5000 for subsequent conviction

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for Being Member of Unlawful Assembly
ipc_sections.description: Whoever is a member of an unlawful assembly shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to six months, or with fine, or with both.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: 6 months imprisonment or fine or both

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for Wrongful Restraint
ipc_sections.description: Whoever wrongfully restrains any person shall be punished with simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to one month, or with fine which may extend to five hundred rupees, or with both.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Simple imprisonment up to 1 month or fine up to Rs. 500 or both

ipc_sections.title: Promoting Enmity Between Different Groups on Ground of Religion, Race, etc.
ipc_sections.description: Whoever by words, either spoken or written, or by signs or by visible representations, promotes or attempts to promote disharmony or feelings of enmity, hatred or ill-will between different religious, racial, language or regional groups or castes or communities.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: 3 years imprisonment or fine or both; 5 years if committed at place of worship

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for Criminal Intimidation
ipc_sections.description: Whoever commits the offence of criminal intimidation shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both; and if the threat be to cause death or grievous hurt, or to cause the destruction of any property by fire, or to cause an offence punishable with death or imprisonment for life, or with imprisonment for a term which may extend to seven years, or to impute unchastity to a woman, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to seven years, or with fine, or with both.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both; 7 years or fine or both if threat is of death or grievous hurt etc.

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for Public Nuisance in Cases Not Otherwise Provided For
ipc_sections.description: Whoever commits a public nuisance in any case not otherwise punishable by this Code.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Fine up to Rs. 200

ipc_sections.title: Continuance of Nuisance After Injunction to Discontinue
ipc_sections.description: Whoever repeats or continues a public nuisance, having been enjoined by any public servant who has lawful authority to issue such injunction not to repeat or continue such nuisance.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Simple imprisonment up to 6 months or fine or both

ipc_sections.title: Various Offenses / Other Clauses
ipc_sections.description: Details not explicitly found in standard directory.

ipc_sections.title: Statements Conducive to Public Mischief
ipc_sections.description: Whoever makes, publishes or circulates any statement, rumour or report with intent to cause, or which is likely to cause, any officer, soldier, sailor or airman in the Army, Navy or Air Force of India to mutiny or disregard his duty; or fear or alarm to the public, or to any section of the public whereby any person may be induced to commit an offence against the State or against the public tranquillity.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years or fine or both

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for Criminal Intimidation
ipc_sections.description: Whoever commits the offence of criminal intimidation shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both; and if the threat be to cause death or grievous hurt, or to cause the destruction of any property by fire, or to cause an offence punishable with death or imprisonment for life, or with imprisonment for a term which may extend to seven years, or to impute unchastity to a woman, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to seven years, or with fine, or with both.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both; 7 years or fine or both if threat is of death or grievous hurt etc.

ipc_sections.title: Disobedience to Order Duly Promulgated by Public Servant
ipc_sections.description: Whoever, knowing that, by an order promulgated by a public servant lawfully empowered to promulgate such order, he is directed to abstain from a certain act or to take certain order with certain property in his possession or under his management, disobeys such direction.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Simple imprisonment up to 1 month or fine up to Rs. 200 or both; 6 months or Rs. 1000 if danger to human life

ipc_sections.title: Punishment of Abetment if Act Abetted Is Committed in Consequence and Where No Provision Is Made
ipc_sections.description: Whoever abets any offence shall, if the act abetted is committed in consequence of the abetment, and no express provision is made for the punishment of such abetment, be punished with the punishment provided for the offence.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Same as for the offence abetted

ipc_sections.title: Punishment of Criminal Conspiracy
ipc_sections.description: Whoever is a party to a criminal conspiracy to commit an offence punishable with death, imprisonment for life or rigorous imprisonment for two years or upwards shall be punished in the same manner as if he had abetted such offence.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Same as for abetment of the offence; imprisonment up to 6 months or fine or both for other conspiracies

ipc_sections.title: Danger or Obstruction in Public Way or Line of Navigation
ipc_sections.description: Whoever, by doing any act, or by omitting to take order with any property in his possession or under his charge, causes danger, obstruction or injury to any person in any public way or public line of navigation.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Fine up to Rs. 200

ipc_sections.title: Voluntarily Causing Grievous Hurt to Extort Property or to Constrain to an Illegal Act
ipc_sections.description: Whoever voluntarily causes grievous hurt for the purpose of extorting from the sufferer or from any person interested in the sufferer any property or valuable security, or of constraining the sufferer or any person interested in such sufferer to do anything that is illegal.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment for life or rigorous imprisonment up to 10 years and fine

sub_sections.description: Whoever commits this offence in order to commit or to facilitate the commission of an offence, using any instrument for shooting, stabbing or cutting or any instrument which, used as a weapon of offence, is likely to cause death, shall be punished with imprisonment for life or rigorous imprisonment up to 10 years and fine.

ipc_sections.title: Abetment of Offence Punishable with Death or Imprisonment for Life — If Offence Not Committed
ipc_sections.description: Whoever abets the commission of an offence punishable with death or imprisonment for life, shall, if that offence be not committed in consequence of the abetment, and no express provision is made for the punishment of such abetment, be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to seven years, and shall also be liable to fine.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years and fine; 14 years and fine if hurt caused

sub_sections.description: If any act for which the abettor is liable in consequence of the abetment, and which causes hurt to any person, is done, the abettor shall be liable to imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to fourteen years, and shall also be liable to fine.

ipc_sections.title: Assault
ipc_sections.description: Whoever makes any gesture, or any preparation intending or knowing it to be likely that such gesture or preparation will cause any person present to apprehend that he who makes that gesture or preparation is about to use criminal force to that person, is said to commit an assault.

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for Wrongful Restraint
ipc_sections.description: Whoever wrongfully restrains any person shall be punished with simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to one month, or with fine which may extend to five hundred rupees, or with both.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Simple imprisonment up to 1 month or fine up to Rs. 500 or both

ipc_sections.title: Obstructing Public Servant in Discharge of Public Functions
ipc_sections.description: Whoever voluntarily obstructs any public servant in the discharge of his public functions.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 months or fine up to Rs. 500 or both

ipc_sections.title: Threat of Injury to Public Servant
ipc_sections.description: Whoever holds out any threat of injury to any public servant, or to any person in whom that public servant is interested, for the purpose of inducing that public servant to do any act, or to forbear or delay to do any act, connected with the exercise of the public functions of such public servant.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both

sub_sections.description: Whoever holds out any threat of injury to any public servant or to any person in whom such public servant is interested, with intent to induce that public servant to do any act connected with the exercise of his public functions and such act is of a criminal nature, shall be punished with imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both.

ipc_sections.title: Continuance of Nuisance After Injunction to Discontinue
ipc_sections.description: Whoever repeats or continues a public nuisance, having been enjoined by any public servant who has lawful authority to issue such injunction not to repeat or continue such nuisance.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Simple imprisonment up to 6 months or fine or both

ipc_sections.title: Various Offenses / Other Clauses
ipc_sections.description: Details not explicitly found in standard directory.

ipc_sections.title: Assault or Criminal Force to Deter Public Servant from Discharge of His Duty
ipc_sections.description: Whoever assaults or uses criminal force to any person being a public servant in the execution of his duty, or with intent to prevent or deter that person from discharging his duty.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for Criminal Intimidation
ipc_sections.description: Whoever commits the offence of criminal intimidation shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both; and if the threat be to cause death or grievous hurt, or to cause the destruction of any property by fire, or to cause an offence punishable with death or imprisonment for life, or with imprisonment for a term which may extend to seven years, or to impute unchastity to a woman, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to seven years, or with fine, or with both.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both; 7 years or fine or both if threat is of death or grievous hurt etc.

ipc_sections.title: Various Offenses / Other Clauses
ipc_sections.description: Details not explicitly found in standard directory.

ipc_sections.title: Voluntarily Causing Hurt by Dangerous Weapons or Means
ipc_sections.description: Whoever, except in the case provided for by section 334, voluntarily causes hurt by means of any instrument for shooting, stabbing or cutting, or any instrument which, used as a weapon of offence, is likely to cause death, or by means of fire or any heated substance, or by means of any poison or any corrosive substance, or by means of any explosive substance.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years or fine or both

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for Criminal Intimidation
ipc_sections.description: Whoever commits the offence of criminal intimidation shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both; and if the threat be to cause death or grievous hurt, or to cause the destruction of any property by fire, or to cause an offence punishable with death or imprisonment for life, or with imprisonment for a term which may extend to seven years, or to impute unchastity to a woman, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to seven years, or with fine, or with both.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both; 7 years or fine or both if threat is of death or grievous hurt etc.

ipc_sections.title: Various Offenses / Other Clauses
ipc_sections.description: Details not explicitly found in standard directory.

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for Voluntarily Causing Hurt
ipc_sections.description: Whoever, except in the case provided for by section 334, voluntarily causes hurt, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to one year, or with fine which may extend to one thousand rupees, or with both.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 1 year or fine up to Rs. 1000 or both

ipc_sections.title: Punishment for Criminal Intimidation
ipc_sections.description: Whoever commits the offence of criminal intimidation shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both; and if the threat be to cause death or grievous hurt, or to cause the destruction of any property by fire, or to cause an offence punishable with death or imprisonment for life, or with imprisonment for a term which may extend to seven years, or to impute unchastity to a woman, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to seven years, or with fine, or with both.
ipc_sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both; 7 years or fine or both if threat is of death or grievous hurt etc.
